funding.title: Graduate Research Assistantship - NLP Lab
funding.description: Graduate Research Assistantships (GRAs) in the NLP Lab include a full tuition waiver and a competitive stipend of $26,500 per academic year in exchange for 20 hours per week of research work. Students are admitted to MS or PhD programs and paired with a faculty advisor.

draft.title: SOP — MS in Artificial Intelligence @ University of Georgia

draft.text:
## Opening

I try to write about my work the way I think about it — plainly, and grounded in what I actually built or measured. I am Alex Rivera, applying to the MS in Artificial Intelligence at University of Georgia for Fall 2026. The program's concentration in Artificial Intelligence and its core-fit curriculum match where I want to spend the next two years. University of Georgia is where this work lives at the scale I need. I want two focused years to go from being an enthusiastic undergraduate researcher to someone who can own a non-trivial research project end to end.

## Research background

I completed my Bachelor of Science in Computer Science at University of Georgia. My cumulative GPA was 3.82 / 4.00.

As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. Designed an ablation over span granularity and built the evaluation harness.

I built a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment that modeled a shared household pantry as a coordination game between independent restocking agents. The interesting part was not the reward function; it was the failure mode. Two agents with almost identical policies... Implemented a multi-agent RL environment in PettingZoo and PyTorch around a pantry-restocking coordination game. Diagnosed a free-rider failure mode and ran an ablation comparing no-communication baselines against a cheap communication channel.

## Why this program

The MS in Artificial Intelligence is structured around the coursework and research I want to be inside of: strong:artificial intelligence, strong:machine learning, strong:deep learning, strong:computer vision. My own focus areas — natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference — map directly onto that menu. I am specifically interested in working with Director of Graduate Studies, whose research in computer vision, machine learning overlaps with the problems I want to work on. Their published work in computer vision is part of what convinced me the fit was real.

## Closing

I am also applying to the Graduate Research Assistantship - NLP Lab, which (per its posting) supports this kind of work and is essential to my plan. I want the next two years to be focused and checkable, and I want advisors who make room for scope-limited experiments over broad, thin projects. I am applying for MS in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence track and I am ready to contribute to the lab on day one.

draft.title: Resume tailoring — MS in Artificial Intelligence @ University of Georgia

claims.text: # Alex Rivera
alex.rivera@example.com
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, University of Georgia (2025) — GPA 3.82 / 4.00.
Tailored for the MS in Artificial Intelligence at University of Georgia.
Emphasis: natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference.
Secondary emphasis: alignment with Director of Graduate Studies (computer vision, machine learning).

- **Retrieval-augmented code search in the UGA NLP Lab.** As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation...
- Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab.
- Designed an ablation over span granularity and built the evaluation harness.
- Authored a span-level error analysis that became part of a workshop paper contribution.

- **Multi-agent RL for pantry restocking.** I built a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment that modeled a shared household pantry as a coordination game between independent restocking agents. The interesting part was not the reward function; it was the failure mode. Two agents with almost identical policies...
- Implemented a multi-agent RL environment in PettingZoo and PyTorch around a pantry-restocking coordination game.
- Diagnosed a free-rider failure mode and ran an ablation comparing no-communication baselines against a cheap communication channel.
- Reported that coordination collapsed without communication and throughput improved modestly with it.

draft.text:
## Header

# Alex Rivera alex.rivera@example.com

## Education

Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, University of Georgia (2025) — GPA 3.82 / 4.00.

## Tailoring summary

Tailored for the MS in Artificial Intelligence at University of Georgia. Emphasis: natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference. Secondary emphasis: alignment with Director of Graduate Studies (computer vision, machine learning).

## Selected experience & projects

- **Retrieval-augmented code search in the UGA NLP Lab.** As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... - Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. - Designed an ablation over span granularity and built the evaluation harness. - Authored a span-level error analysis that became part of a workshop paper contribution.

- **Multi-agent RL for pantry restocking.** I built a multi-agent reinforcement learning environment that modeled a shared household pantry as a coordination game between independent restocking agents. The interesting part was not the reward function; it was the failure mode. Two agents with almost identical policies... - Implemented a multi-agent RL environment in PettingZoo and PyTorch around a pantry-restocking coordination game. - Diagnosed a free-rider failure mode and ran an ablation comparing no-communication baselines against a cheap communication channel. - Reported that coordination collapsed without communication and throughput improved modestly with it.

draft.title: Personal statement — MS in Artificial Intelligence @ University of Georgia

claims.text: I write about my own work the way I think about it: one argument per paragraph, nothing said that cannot be shown.
My name is Alex Rivera, and my path to the MS in Artificial Intelligence started with small, concrete projects that forced me to be specific about what I actually changed.
I trained at University of Georgia, and the projects that stuck with me were the ones where I had to defend the choices I made.

As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation...
Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab.
I want two focused years to go from being an enthusiastic undergraduate researcher to someone who can own a non-trivial research project end to end.
The next two years are about focused work on natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference.
The MS in Artificial Intelligence is where that kind of scoped, checkable work lives.
That is why I am applying to University of Georgia.

draft.text:
## Personal statement

I write about my own work the way I think about it: one argument per paragraph, nothing said that cannot be shown. My name is Alex Rivera, and my path to the MS in Artificial Intelligence started with small, concrete projects that forced me to be specific about what I actually changed.

I trained at University of Georgia, and the projects that stuck with me were the ones where I had to defend the choices I made. As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. I want two focused years to go from being an enthusiastic undergraduate researcher to someone who can own a non-trivial research project end to end.

The next two years are about focused work on natural language processing, multi-agent reinforcement learning, on-device inference. The MS in Artificial Intelligence is where that kind of scoped, checkable work lives. That is why I am applying to University of Georgia.

draft.title: Cover letter — Graduate Research Assistantship - NLP Lab

claims.text: Dear Priya Sharma,
I am writing in plain sentences because I want the ask to be easy to check.
I am applying to the Graduate Research Assistantship - NLP Lab at University of Georgia.
I am Alex Rivera, a candidate for the MS in Artificial Intelligence starting Fall 2026.
The MS in Artificial Intelligence concentration is Artificial Intelligence, which is what made this funding route the right one to pursue.
I am specifically interested in working with the Director of Graduate Studies on computer vision, machine learning.

As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation...
Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab.
I have read the group's public work and it is directly connected to the problem I just described.
I would like to be considered for this assistantship in tandem with my MS in Artificial Intelligence application.
I can commit to the funding's time expectation as described on the posting.
I can be reached at alex.rivera@example.com.
Thank you for considering this application for MS in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence track.

draft.text:
Dear Priya Sharma,

I am writing in plain sentences because I want the ask to be easy to check. I am applying to the Graduate Research Assistantship - NLP Lab at University of Georgia. I am Alex Rivera, a candidate for the MS in Artificial Intelligence starting Fall 2026. The MS in Artificial Intelligence concentration is Artificial Intelligence, which is what made this funding route the right one to pursue.

I am specifically interested in working with the Director of Graduate Studies on computer vision, machine learning. As an undergraduate researcher in the UGA NLP Lab I led a retrieval-augmented code search project that asked a narrow question: does span-level supervision meaningfully improve retrieval on low-resource code repositories? I built the evaluation harness, designed an ablation... Led retrieval-augmented code search project end to end as an undergraduate researcher at the UGA NLP Lab. I have read the group's public work and it is directly connected to the problem I just described.

I would like to be considered for this assistantship in tandem with my MS in Artificial Intelligence application. I can commit to the funding's time expectation as described on the posting. I can be reached at alex.rivera@example.com. Thank you for considering this application for MS in Computer Science / Artificial Intelligence track.
